mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which directly translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return following periods of positive RSAFS growth is not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a tightening labor market, which typically leads to increased consumer confidence and spending, ultimately boosting corporate profits and driving S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE drops by more than 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of low unemployment with economic growth
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops by more than 0.2pp during an expansionary period.

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) are low, it indicates an accommodative monetary environment with low borrowing costs and stable purchasing power, which reduces the discount rate for future earnings and supports higher equity valuations over a monthly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low rates reduce cost of capital
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to generate positive 20-day returns in at least 60% of periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL meet the low-level criteria.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals growing investment and consumer confidence in the housing sector, which typically precedes broader economic recovery and increased demand for goods and services, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:housing as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) during periods identified as post-recession recovery when HOUST shows sustained increases.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening manufacturing activity and potential economic contraction, which directly impacts corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines by more than 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:industrial production as a coincident economic indicator
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances where INDPRO declines by over 0.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate revenues, driving positive equity market performance over a short-term, weekly horizon.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return is not positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) during periods of sustained PAYEMS growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) when inflation (CPIAUCSL) is already elevated signals aggressive monetary tightening, which significantly raises borrowing costs and reduces future earnings expectations, leading to negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% in a single month AND CPIAUCSL annual change > 3.0%
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 30% of triggered instances during a high inflation, tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates a healthy expanding economy, which generally translates into higher corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon, reflecting overall economic prosperity.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP as a primary measure of economic health
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return is not positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05) during periods where GDPC1 shows positive quarter-over-quarter growth.
